   237Рєњ## 2.4 C++ Optimizer Stream Protocol
   238Рєњ
   239Рєњ### 2.4.1 Stream Architecture
   240Рєњ
   241Рєњ```
   242РєњРћїРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћљ         NDJSON Stream          РћїРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћљ
   243РєњРћѓ                 Рћѓ РћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРќ║ Рћѓ                 Рћѓ
   244РєњРћѓ   Bun Worker    Рћѓ         stdin (input)          Рћѓ  mms-optimizer  Рћѓ
   245РєњРћѓ   (TypeScript)  Рћѓ                                Рћѓ     (C++)       Рћѓ
   246РєњРћѓ                 Рћѓ РЌёРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђ Рћѓ                 Рћѓ
   247РєњРћѓ                 Рћѓ    stdout (results, NDJSON)    Рћѓ                 Рћѓ
   248РєњРћѓ                 Рћѓ РЌёРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђ Рћѓ                 Рћѓ
   249РєњРћѓ                 Рћѓ    stderr (logs, metrics)      Рћѓ                 Рћѓ
   250РєњРћћРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћў                                РћћРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћђРћў
   251Рєњ```
   252Рєњ
   253Рєњ### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema
   254Рєњ
   255Рєњ```typescript
   256Рєњinterface OptimizerInput {
   257Рєњ  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
   258Рєњ  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
   259Рєњ  config: {
   260Рєњ    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
   261Рєњ    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
   262Рєњ    soft_constraint_penalties: {
   263Рєњ      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
   264Рєњ      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
   265Рєњ      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
   266Рєњ    };
   267Рєњ    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
   268Рєњ  };
   269Рєњ  
   270Рєњ  trains: {
   271Рєњ    id: string;
   272Рєњ    train_number: string;
   273Рєњ    fleet_id: string;
   274Рєњ    current_mileage: number;
   275Рєњ    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
   276Рєњ    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
   277Рєњ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
   278Рєњ    home_depot_id: string;
   279Рєњ    current_depot_id: string;
   280Рєњ  }[];
   281Рєњ  
   282Рєњ  routes: {
   283Рєњ    id: string;
   284Рєњ    route_code: string;
   285Рєњ    expected_km: number;
   286Рєњ    required_trains: number;
   287Рєњ    allowed_fleets: string[];
   288Рєњ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
   289Рєњ  }[];
   290Рєњ  
   291Рєњ  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
   292Рєњ  hard_constraints: {
   293Рєњ    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
   294Рєњ    train_id: string;
   295Рєњ    description: string;
   296Рєњ  }[];
   297Рєњ  
   298Рєњ  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
   299Рєњ  soft_constraints: {
   300Рєњ    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
   301Рєњ    train_id: string;
   302Рєњ    penalty_weight: number;
   303Рєњ    description: string;
   304Рєњ  }[];
   305Рєњ  
   306Рєњ  // Material availability (NEW)
   307Рєњ  material_positions: {
   308Рєњ    depot_id: string;
   309Рєњ    part_number: string;
   310Рєњ    quantity_available: number;  // on_hand - reserved
   311Рєњ    next_delivery_date: string | null;
   312Рєњ  }[];
   313Рєњ  
   314Рєњ  // PM material requirements (NEW)
   315Рєњ  pending_pms: {
   316Рєњ    pm_id: string;
   317Рєњ    train_id: string;
   318Рєњ    pm_type: string;
   319Рєњ    target_date: string;
   320Рєњ    required_materials: {
   321Рєњ      part_number: string;
   322Рєњ      quantity: number;
   323Рєњ      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
   324Рєњ    }[];
   325Рєњ  }[];
   326Рєњ}
   327Рєњ```
   328Рєњ
   329Рєњ### 2.4.3 Output Message Schema
   330Рєњ
   331Рєњ```typescript
   332Рєњ// Progress updates (streamed during solve)
   333Рєњinterface ProgressMessage {
   334Рєњ  type: 'PROGRESS';
   335Рєњ  request_id: string;
   336Рєњ  percent: number;
   337Рєњ  current_objective: number;
   338Рєњ  feasible: boolean;
   339Рєњ  elapsed_ms: number;
   340Рєњ}
   341Рєњ
   342Рєњ// Final result
   343Рєњinterface ResultMessage {
   344Рєњ  type: 'RESULT';
   345Рєњ  request_id: string;
   346Рєњ  status: 'OPTIMAL' | 'FEASIBLE' | 'FALLBACK' | 'INFEASIBLE';
   347Рєњ  solve_time_ms: number;
   348Рєњ  
   349Рєњ  assignments: {
   350Рєњ    train_id: string;
   351Рєњ    route_id: string;
   352Рєњ    score: number;
   353Рєњ    
   354Рєњ    // NEW: Explanation trace
   355Рєњ    explanation: {
   356Рєњ      constraint_type: 'HARD' | 'SOFT';
   357Рєњ      constraint_name: string;
   358Рєњ      impact: number;  // Contribution to objective
   359Рєњ      description: string;  // Human-readable
   360Рєњ    }[];
   361Рєњ  }[];
   362Рєњ  
   363Рєњ  // Trains not assigned (with reasons)
   364Рєњ  unassigned: {
   365Рєњ    train_id: string;
   366Рєњ    reason: string;
   367Рєњ    blocking_constraints: string[];
   368Рєњ  }[];
   369Рєњ  
   370Рєњ  // Material warnings (NEW)
   371Рєњ  material_warnings: {
   372Рєњ    pm_id: string;
   373Рєњ    train_id: string;
   374Рєњ    part_number: string;
   375Рєњ    status: 'UNAVAILABLE' | 'LOW_STOCK' | 'ARRIVING_LATE';
   376Рєњ    description: string;
   377Рєњ  }[];
   378Рєњ  
   379Рєњ  summary: {
   380Рєњ    total_penalty: number;
   381Рєњ    trains_assigned: number;
   382Рєњ    trains_unassigned: number;
   383Рєњ    soft_constraint_breakdown: Record<string, number>;
   384Рєњ    material_blocks: number;
   385Рєњ  };
   386Рєњ}
   387Рєњ
   388Рєњ// Metrics (to stderr, OpenTelemetry format)
   389Рєњinterface MetricsMessage {
   390Рєњ  _type: 'metrics';
   391Рєњ  timestamp: number;
   392Рєњ  metrics: {
   393Рєњ    'optimizer.solve_time_ms': number;
   394Рєњ    'optimizer.branches_explored': number;
   395Рєњ    'optimizer.conflicts_found': number;
   396Рєњ    'optimizer.propagations': number;
   397Рєњ    'optimizer.trains_assigned': number;
   398Рєњ    'optimizer.soft_violations': number;
   399Рєњ    'optimizer.material_blocks': number;
   400Рєњ  };
   401Рєњ  tags: {
   402Рєњ    status: string;
   403Рєњ    depot_id: string;
   404Рєњ  };
   405Рєњ}
   406Рєњ
   407Рєњ// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
   408Рєњ// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
   409Рєњinterface ErrorMessage {
   410Рєњ  type: 'ERROR';
   411Рєњ  request_id: string;
   412Рєњ  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
   413Рєњ  error_message: string;
   414Рєњ  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
   415Рєњ  last_progress?: {
   416Рєњ    percent: number;
   417Рєњ    branches_explored: number;
   418Рєњ    memory_mb: number;
   419Рєњ  };
   420Рєњ  recovery_suggestion: string;  // Human-readable
   421Рєњ}
   422Рєњ
   423Рєњ// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
   424Рєњinterface HeartbeatMessage {
   425Рєњ  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
   426Рєњ  request_id: string;
   427Рєњ  timestamp: number;
   428Рєњ  memory_mb: number;
   429Рєњ  thread_count: number;
   430Рєњ  branches_per_second: number;
   431Рєњ}
   432Рєњ```
   433Рєњ
   434Рєњ### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435Рєњ
   436Рєњ**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437Рєњ
   438Рєњ```typescript
   439Рєњ// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440Рєњclass OptimizerWorker {
   441Рєњ  private process: ChildProcess;
   442Рєњ  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443Рєњ  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444Рєњ  
   445Рєњ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446Рєњ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447Рєњ  
   448Рєњ  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449Рєњ    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450Рєњ      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451Рєњ      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452Рєњ      
   453Рєњ      // Handle stream events
   454Рєњ      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455Рєњ        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456Рєњ          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457Рєњ          
   458Рєњ          try {
   459Рєњ            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460Рєњ            
   461Рєњ            switch (msg.type) {
   462Рєњ              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463Рєњ                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464Рєњ                break;
   465Рєњ                
   466Рєњ              case 'PROGRESS':
   467Рєњ                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468Рєњ                break;
   469Рєњ                
   470Рєњ              case 'RESULT':
   471Рєњ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472Рєњ                resolve(msg);
   473Рєњ                break;
   474Рєњ                
   475Рєњ              case 'ERROR':
   476Рєњ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477Рєњ                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478Рєњ                break;
   479Рєњ            }
   480Рєњ          } catch (parseError) {
   481Рєњ            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482Рєњ          }
   483Рєњ        }
   484Рєњ      });
   485Рєњ      
   486Рєњ      // Handle process crash
   487Рєњ      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488Рєњ        if (code !== 0) {
   489Рєњ          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490Рєњ          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491Рєњ            type: 'ERROR',
   492Рєњ            request_id: request.request_id,
   493Рєњ            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494Рєњ            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495Рєњ            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496Рєњ          }));
   497Рєњ        }
   498Рєњ      });
   499Рєњ      
   500Рєњ      // Send request
   501Рєњ      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502Рєњ    });
   503Рєњ  }
   504Рєњ  
   505Рєњ  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506Рєњ    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507Рєњ    
   508Рєњ    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509Рєњ      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510Рєњ      
   511Рєњ      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512Рєњ        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513Рєњ        
   514Рєњ        // Kill hung process
   515Рєњ        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516Рєњ        
   517Рєњ        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518Рєњ          type: 'ERROR',
   519Рєњ          request_id: requestId,
   520Рєњ          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521Рєњ          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522Рєњ          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523Рєњ        }));
   524Рєњ      }
   525Рєњ    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526Рєњ  }
   527Рєњ  
   528Рєњ  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529Рєњ    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530Рєњ      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531Рєњ      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532Рєњ    }
   533Рєњ  }
   534Рєњ}
   535Рєњ
   536Рєњclass OptimizerError extends Error {
   537Рєњ  readonly errorCode: string;
   538Рєњ  readonly requestId: string;
   539Рєњ  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540Рєњ  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541Рєњ  
   542Рєњ  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543Рєњ    super(msg.error_message);
   544Рєњ    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545Рєњ    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546Рєњ    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547Рєњ    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548Рєњ    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549Рєњ  }
   550Рєњ}
   551Рєњ```
   552Рєњ
   553Рєњ**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554Рєњ
   555РєњThe C++ binary must:
   556Рєњ1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557Рєњ2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558Рєњ3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559Рєњ4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560Рєњ
   561Рєњ```cpp
   562Рєњ// Signal handler example (C++)
   563Рєњvoid signalHandler(int signal) {
   564Рєњ    nlohmann::json error;
   565Рєњ    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566Рєњ    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567Рєњ    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568Рєњ    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569Рєњ    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570Рєњ    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571Рєњ    
   572Рєњ    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573Рєњ    std::cout.flush();
   574Рєњ    
   575Рєњ    _exit(128 + signal);
   576Рєњ}
   577Рєњ```
   578Рєњ
   579РєњThe Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580Рєњ
   581Рєњ### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582Рєњ
   583РєњThe Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584Рєњ
   585Рєњ### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586Рєњ
   587Рєњ```typescript
   588Рєњinterface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589Рєњ  period: {
   590Рєњ    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591Рєњ    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592Рєњ  };
   593Рєњ  
   594Рєњ  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595Рєњ  track_capacity: {
   596Рєњ    depot_id: string;
   597Рєњ    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598Рєњ    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599Рєњ    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600Рєњ    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601Рєњ  }[];
   602Рєњ  
   603Рєњ  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604Рєњ  labor_capacity: {
   605Рєњ    depot_id: string;
   606Рєњ    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607Рєњ    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608Рєњ    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609Рєњ    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610Рєњ  }[];
   611Рєњ  
   612Рєњ  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613Рєњ  material_capacity: {
   614Рєњ    depot_id: string;
   615Рєњ    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616Рєњ    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617Рєњ    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618Рєњ    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619Рєњ  }[];
   620Рєњ  
   621Рєњ  // Constraints from tactical model
   622Рєњ  blocked_trains: {
   623Рєњ    train_id: string;
   624Рєњ    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625Рєњ    reason: string;
   626Рєњ  }[];
   627Рєњ}
   628Рєњ
   629Рєњclass ConstraintAggregationService {
   630Рєњ  /**
   631Рєњ   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632Рєњ   */
   633Рєњ  async generateEnvelope(
   634Рєњ    quarterStart: Date,
   635Рєњ    quarterEnd: Date,
   636Рєњ    depotIds: string[]
   637Рєњ  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638Рєњ    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639Рєњ    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640Рєњ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641Рєњ    );
   642Рєњ    
   643Рєњ    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644Рєњ    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645Рєњ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646Рєњ    );
   647Рєњ    
   648Рєњ    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649Рєњ    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650Рєњ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651Рєњ    );
   652Рєњ    
   653Рєњ    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654Рєњ    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655Рєњ      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656Рєњ    );
   657Рєњ    
   658Рєњ    return {
   659Рєњ      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660Рєњ      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661Рєњ      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662Рєњ      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663Рєњ      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664Рєњ    };
   665Рєњ  }
   666Рєњ  
   667Рєњ  /**
   668Рєњ   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669Рєњ   */
   670Рєњ  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671Рєњ    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672Рєњ    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673Рєњ  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674Рєњ    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675Рєњ    
   676Рєњ    // Check track capacity
   677Рєњ    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678Рєњ      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679Рєњ        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680Рєњ             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681Рєњ      );
   682Рєњ      
   683Рєњ      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684Рєњ        violations.push({
   685Рєњ          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686Рєњ          severity: 'HARD',
   687Рєњ          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688Рєњ        });
   689Рєњ      }
   690Рєњ    }
   691Рєњ    
   692Рєњ    // Check labor capacity
   693Рєњ    // Check material capacity
   694Рєњ    // ... similar validation logic
   695Рєњ    
   696Рєњ    return {
   697Рєњ      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698Рєњ      violations
   699Рєњ    };
   700Рєњ  }
   701Рєњ}
   702Рєњ```
   703Рєњ
   704Рєњ---
   705Рєњ
   706Рєњ## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707Рєњ
   708Рєњ### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709Рєњ
   710РєњEvery optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711Рєњ
   712Рєњ- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713Рєњ- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714Рєњ- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715Рєњ- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716Рєњ
   717Рєњ### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718Рєњ
   719РєњThe system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720Рєњ
   721Рєњ- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722Рєњ- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723Рєњ- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724Рєњ- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725Рєњ
   726Рєњ### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727Рєњ
   728РєњFor 12-year government contract compliance:
   729Рєњ
   730Рєњ- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731Рєњ- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732Рєњ- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733Рєњ- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734Рєњ
   735Рєњ### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736Рєњ
   737РєњPrevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738Рєњ
   739Рєњ- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740Рєњ- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741Рєњ- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742Рєњ- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743Рєњ

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 2: System Architecture

**Document Version:** 2.0

---

## 2.1 High-Level Architecture

```

Рћѓ                                    CLIENTS                                       Рћѓ

## 2.2 Component Responsibilities

### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |

### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |

### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |

### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers

| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
|--------|----------------|-------------|
| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |

### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |

## 2.3 Critical Data Flows

### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow

```
06:00 Рћѓ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) Рєњ File Watcher detects
06:05 Рћѓ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
06:10 Рћѓ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
06:15 Рћѓ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
06:30 Рћѓ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
      Рћѓ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
      Рћѓ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
07:00 Рћѓ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
07:00 Рћѓ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
      Рћѓ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
      Рћѓ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
08:00 Рћѓ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
16:00 Рћѓ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
22:00 Рћѓ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
```

### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow

```
Monday 06:00  Рћѓ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
              Рћѓ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
              Рћѓ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
Monday 06:30  Рћѓ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
              Рћѓ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
              Рћѓ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
Monday 07:00  Рћѓ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
              Рћѓ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
              Рћѓ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
Monday 08:00  Рћѓ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
Tuesday       Рћѓ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
              Рћѓ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
              Рћѓ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
Wednesday    Рћѓ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
Friday       Рћѓ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
```

### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow

```
User Action  Рћѓ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
             Рћѓ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
             Рћѓ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
API Call     Рћѓ POST /api/v1/baselines
             Рћѓ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
             Рћѓ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
             Рћѓ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
             Рћѓ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
Post-Publish Рћѓ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
             Рћѓ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
```

### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow

```
User Action  Рћѓ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
             Рћѓ   - System forks from current published baseline
             Рћѓ   - New scenario_id assigned
Editing      Рћѓ Planner makes changes within scenario
             Рћѓ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
             Рћѓ   - Original baseline data unchanged
Comparison   Рћѓ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
             Рћѓ   - System materializes both scenarios
             Рћѓ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
             Рћѓ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
Promotion    Рћѓ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
             Рћѓ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
             Рћѓ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
```

### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema

```typescript
interface OptimizerInput {
  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
  config: {
    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
    soft_constraint_penalties: {
      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
    };
    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
  };
  
  trains: {
    id: string;
    train_number: string;
    fleet_id: string;
    current_mileage: number;
    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
    home_depot_id: string;
    current_depot_id: string;
  }[];
  
  routes: {
    id: string;
    route_code: string;
    expected_km: number;
    required_trains: number;
    allowed_fleets: string[];
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
  }[];
  
  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
  hard_constraints: {
    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
    train_id: string;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
  soft_constraints: {
    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
    train_id: string;
    penalty_weight: number;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Material availability (NEW)
  material_positions: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_number: string;
    quantity_available: number;  // on_hand - reserved
    next_delivery_date: string | null;
  }[];
  
  // PM material requirements (NEW)
  pending_pms: {
    pm_id: string;
    train_id: string;
    pm_type: string;
    target_date: string;
    required_materials: {
      part_number: string;
      quantity: number;
      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
    }[];
  }[];
}
```

### 2.4.3 Output Message Schema

```typescript
// Progress updates (streamed during solve)
interface ProgressMessage {
  type: 'PROGRESS';
  request_id: string;
  percent: number;
  current_objective: number;
  feasible: boolean;
  elapsed_ms: number;
}

// Final result
interface ResultMessage {
  type: 'RESULT';
  request_id: string;
  status: 'OPTIMAL' | 'FEASIBLE' | 'FALLBACK' | 'INFEASIBLE';
  solve_time_ms: number;
  
  assignments: {
    train_id: string;
    route_id: string;
    score: number;
    
    // NEW: Explanation trace
    explanation: {
      constraint_type: 'HARD' | 'SOFT';
      constraint_name: string;
      impact: number;  // Contribution to objective
      description: string;  // Human-readable
    }[];
  }[];
  
  // Trains not assigned (with reasons)
  unassigned: {
    train_id: string;
    reason: string;
    blocking_constraints: string[];
  }[];
  
  // Material warnings (NEW)
  material_warnings: {
    pm_id: string;
    train_id: string;
    part_number: string;
    status: 'UNAVAILABLE' | 'LOW_STOCK' | 'ARRIVING_LATE';
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  summary: {
    total_penalty: number;
    trains_assigned: number;
    trains_unassigned: number;
    soft_constraint_breakdown: Record<string, number>;
    material_blocks: number;
  };
}

// Metrics (to stderr, OpenTelemetry format)
interface MetricsMessage {
  _type: 'metrics';
  timestamp: number;
  metrics: {
    'optimizer.solve_time_ms': number;
    'optimizer.branches_explored': number;
    'optimizer.conflicts_found': number;
    'optimizer.propagations': number;
    'optimizer.trains_assigned': number;
    'optimizer.soft_violations': number;
    'optimizer.material_blocks': number;
  };
  tags: {
    status: string;
    depot_id: string;
  };
}

// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
interface ErrorMessage {
  type: 'ERROR';
  request_id: string;
  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
  error_message: string;
  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
  last_progress?: {
    percent: number;
    branches_explored: number;
    memory_mb: number;
  };
  recovery_suggestion: string;  // Human-readable
}

// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
interface HeartbeatMessage {
  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
  request_id: string;
  timestamp: number;
  memory_mb: number;
  thread_count: number;
  branches_per_second: number;
}
```

### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol

**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.

```typescript
// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
class OptimizerWorker {
  private process: ChildProcess;
  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
  
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
  
  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      // Start heartbeat monitor
      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
      
      // Handle stream events
      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
          if (!line.trim()) continue;
          
          try {
            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
            
            switch (msg.type) {
              case 'HEARTBEAT':
                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
                break;
                
              case 'PROGRESS':
                this.emitProgress(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'RESULT':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                resolve(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'ERROR':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
                break;
            }
          } catch (parseError) {
            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
          }
        }
      });
      
      // Handle process crash
      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
        if (code !== 0) {
          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
          reject(new OptimizerError({
            type: 'ERROR',
            request_id: request.request_id,
            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
          }));
        }
      });
      
      // Send request
      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
    });
  }
  
  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
    
    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
      
      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
        
        // Kill hung process
        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
        
        reject(new OptimizerError({
          type: 'ERROR',
          request_id: requestId,
          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
        }));
      }
    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
  }
  
  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
    }
  }
}

class OptimizerError extends Error {
  readonly errorCode: string;
  readonly requestId: string;
  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
  readonly stackTrace?: string;
  
  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
    super(msg.error_message);
    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
  }
}
```

**C++ Implementation Notes:**

The C++ binary must:
1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)

```cpp
// Signal handler example (C++)
void signalHandler(int signal) {
    nlohmann::json error;
    error["type"] = "ERROR";
    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
    
    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
    std::cout.flush();
    
    _exit(128 + signal);
}
```

The Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.

### 2.5.1 Purpose

The Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.

### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process

```typescript
interface ConstraintEnvelope {
  period: {
    start: Date;  // Quarter start
    end: Date;    // Quarter end
  };
  
  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
  track_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
  labor_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
  material_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
  }[];
  
  // Constraints from tactical model
  blocked_trains: {
    train_id: string;
    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
    reason: string;
  }[];
}

class ConstraintAggregationService {
  /**
   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   */
  async generateEnvelope(
    quarterStart: Date,
    quarterEnd: Date,
    depotIds: string[]
  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
    // 1. Aggregate track availability
    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 3. Aggregate material projections
    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd
    );
    
    return {
      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
    };
  }
  
  /**
   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   */
  async validateStrategicAllocation(
    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
    
    // Check track capacity
    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
      );
      
      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
        violations.push({
          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
          severity: 'HARD',
          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
        });
      }
    }
    
    // Check labor capacity
    // Check material capacity
    // ... similar validation logic
    
    return {
      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
      violations
    };
  }
}
```

---

## 2.6 Key Design Principles

### 2.6.1 Explainability First

Every optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:

- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used

### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation

The system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:

- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records

### 2.6.3 Audit Everything

For 12-year government contract compliance:

- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved

### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning

Prevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:

- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
